subject: Biology
title: Cancer as Evolution Inside the Body
description: A quick, practical guide to Cancer as Evolution Inside the Body.

hook.text: Cancer is natural selection happening inside your body—mutant cells competing for resources.

buildup.text: Cancer cells accumulate mutations that let them grow faster, dodge immune cells, and recruit blood vessels.

discovery.text: Tumors are diverse populations—different clones compete, and the fittest clones expand.

twist.text: Chemotherapy kills susceptible cells but selects for resistant ones—an evolutionary arms race inside a patient.

climax.text: Adaptive therapy gives lower drug doses to keep drug-sensitive cells alive—they outcompete resistant ones.

punchline.text: Cancer evolves. Treatment must evolve with it.

quiz.question: Why can chemotherapy sometimes fail over time?
options: It selects for drug-resistant cancer cell clones
The drugs expire inside the body
The patient becomes immune to the drug
